subject: [PATCH] vfs: remove externs from fs.h on functions modified by i_ino widening

reviews.author: Mateusz Guzik
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mateusz Guzik suggested removing all extern keywords from fs.h, questioning the need for a more selective approach.
reviews.raw_body:
Is there a reason to not straight up whack the keyword from *all* funcs in
the file?

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary: Reviewer Darrick Wong expressed disappointment that the removal of kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro was done despite checkpatch tool's recommendation against adding new typedefs, which he believes would have made it clear when inode numbers are being used.
reviews.raw_body:
I think inode numbers ought to be their own typedef to make it *really
obvious* when you're dealing with one, and was pretty sad to see "vfs:
remove kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro" so soon after one was
added.  But our really excellent checkpatch tool says "do not add new
typedefs" so instead everyone else has to be really smart about what
"u64" represents when they see one, particularly because arithmetic is
meaningless for this particular "u64".

Yay.

--D

2026-03-06.patch_summary: This patch removes the 'extern' keyword from several functions in fs.h that were modified by a previous patch to widen the type of inode numbers (i_ino widening series). The removal of these externs is suggested by Christoph Hellwig as it makes the code cleaner and more consistent. The patch updates 17 function declarations in include/linux/fs.h, removing the extern keyword from each one.

reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Matthew Wilcox suggested renaming the unnamed `u64` parameter to make its purpose clear, specifically questioning whether it represents `i_ino` or `hashval`, and expressed concern that unnamed parameters can be confusing.

Reviewer Matthew Wilcox requested reformatting of function prototypes in fs.h to improve readability, specifically suggesting alignment with the open bracket and providing a descriptive name for the u64 type.
reviews.raw_body:
I think plain 'u64' deserves a name.  I know some people get very
upset when they see any unnamed parameter, but I don't think that you
need to put "sb" in the first parameter.  A u64 is non-obvious though;
is it i_ino?  Or hashval?

---

I think these need to be reflowed.  Before they were aligned with the
open bracket, and this demonstrates why that's a stupid convention.
And the u64 needs a name.

(other occurrences snipped)

2026-03-07.patch_summary: This patch removes the 'extern' keyword from several functions in fs.h that were modified by a previous patch to widen the type of inode numbers (i_ino widening series). The removal of these externs is suggested by Christoph Hellwig as it makes the code cleaner and more consistent. The patch updates 17 function declarations in include/linux/fs.h, removing the extern keyword from each one.
